category_title: Emotionally disturbed first-grader with trauma-induced behavioral challenges
minimal_context: Jamal is asked to practice the basic alphabet while sitting in a classroom with 25 other students.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Jamal, it is time to do the basic alphabet.

STUDENT: (Shows wobbly pouting lips) I don't want to do the letters.

TUTOR: We need to finish this assignment.

STUDENT: (Looks around at the other students, loudly seeking attention) No!

TUTOR: Please just try to write the first few letters.

STUDENT: (Crying) I can’t do it!

options.A: Have support personnel, such as a teacher’s assistant or paraprofessional, present in the classroom to assist the student during the alphabet activity.
options.B: Use large fonts with supporting pictures, reduce the number of alphabet letters to practice, provide access to word banks, and use speech-generating devices.
options.C: Ensure that teaching assistants do not routinely support the student, and address any issues of student separation from the class, teacher, and peers.
options.D: Combine photo elicitation with life-story splicing of narrative inquiry, sharing photographs of the student and their family, and sharing stories about these photographs.
